full_text:
t-CyCIF protocol

Day 0

Preparations

Make sure that the tissue section being stained is not older than 6 months and has been stored at +40C.

Set the oven’s temperature to 560C (double check the temperature using a thermometer).

Check the levels of xylene and alcohol in the dewaxing and rehydration series in the fume hood, if the levels are too low add more xylene or respective alcohol. Prepare fresh xylene and alcohol series after using them 5 times.

Make sure that all the buffers and antibodies are available: antigen retrieval (100mM citric acid pH 6.0), odyssey blocking buffer, 30% H202, & 480mM NaOH, and 1x PBS.

Day 1 (approx. 6.5 h)

Dewaxing and antigen retrieval (approx. 2h 10 min)

Baking: place the slides vertically into a grey plastic slide holder and bake in a 560C oven for 30 min.

Deparaffinization + rehydration:

3 x 5 min Xylene

1 x 3 min 100% ethanol

1 x 3 min 90% ethanol

1 x 3 min 70% ethanol

1 x 3 min 50% ethanol

1 x 3 min 30% ethanol

2 x 1 min successive 1x PBS solution

Antigen retrieval:

Always use freshly prepared citric acid buffer, 10 mM, pH 6.0:

From 100mM stock 🡪 25mL of stock solution + 225mL of MQ.

100 mM citric acid 1L:

Dissolve 21.0 grams of citric acid monohydrate (Sigma #C1909) to 800 mL of MQ H20.

Add 9.94 grams of NaOH pellets (Merck # 1310-73-2)

Leave on a magnetic stirrer to thoroughly mix

Measure the pH and set to 6.0

Level the volume up to 1L with MQ H20.

Note: Use the solution not more than 6 months after preparation

Transfer the slides onto a white plastic holder and in a glass dish - fill with 250 mL citric acid buffer.

Boil sections in a microwave at 600 W as follows:

Boil for 4 min - leave the slides inside for 5 min.

Boil for 2 min - leave the slides inside for 5 min.

Boil for 2 min.

Let to cool down for 20 mins RT

Wash three times with 1x PBS, ~ 3 min each.

2. Pre-staining (approx. 2h)

Prepare a moist incubation chamber to minimize evaporation: Wet regular tissue paper under the tap water and fold it to fit inside the grey slide box. After cooling and washing from step 1.3., take out the slides one at a time, dry the surroundings of the tissue section using soft fiber-free tissue paper. Without letting the tissue dry, quickly add 200-300 uL odyssey blocking buffer per section and incubate for 30 min RT. The use of PAP pen is not needed. Do not spread the blocking buffer with a pipette tip, as you might scratch the tissue while doing so. In addition, thorough drying of the surroundings of the tissue creates a hydrophobic surface so that the solution does not spread outside the tissue.

After the incubation, remove the blocking buffer by vertically tapping the slide on a tissue paper. Next, pre-stain the slides with secondary antibodies and Hoechst only by adding 200-300uL of pre-diluted antibodies per section. Incubate for 60 min at RT in a moist chamber (from 2.1.).

Prepare cocktail of secondary antibodies and Hoechst in odyssey blocking buffer:

488 goat anti-rabbit 1:1000

555 donkey anti-goat 1:1000

647 goat anti-mouse 1:1000

HOECHST 1:5000

Transfer the slides into a vertical grey plastic holder placed in a white plastic container with 1xPBS. Wash three times with 1x PBS, ~ 3 min each.

3. Bleaching/Fluorophore inactivation (approx. 2h)

Prepare 50 mL bleaching solution in a falcon tube as follows:

40 ml 1xPBS

7.5ml 30% H2O2

2.5ml 480 mM NaOH

Pour the bleaching solution into a white slide box lid placed on top of a LED light.

Using forceps, transfer the slides into a bleaching solution; make sure that the slides are completely immersed in the solution. Place another LED light on top of the slide box (step 3.2). Connect bottom and top LED lights to power.

Photobleach the sides for 90 min at RT, during which change the buffer solution after every 30 min.

Wash four times in PBS, ~ 3 min each.

NOTE: Only for cycles 0 and 1 change the bleaching solution 2x after every 30 min. The rest of the cycles are only 1x after 45 min.

4. Staining with secondaries and Hoechst (approx. 15 min)

4.1. During the washing step from 3.4., prepare a cocktail of secondary antibodies and Hoechst in

odyssey blocking buffer:

488 goat anti-rabbit 1:2000

555 donkey anti-goat 1:2000

647 goat anti-mouse 1:2000

HOECHST 1:10 000

NOTE: dilutions are different from step 2.2.

4.2. Add 200-300uL per section and incubate at +4°C o/n in a moist chamber (from 2.1.)

Day 2

Wash the slides three times in 1x PBS, ~ 3 min each

Dry the surroundings of the tissue section using soft fiber-free tissue paper. Add 100 uL of 70%

glycerol per section and carefully cover with a coverslip, avoiding air bubbles.

Scan the slides.

NOTE: Remove excess glycerol from the surrounding of the slides; the slide holder will become dirty if the slide edges are moist and contain excess glycerol.

De-coverslipping

5.1. Prewarm 1x PBS to about +40°C.

5.2. Using the grey slide holder, immerse the slides vertically into the prewarmed 1x PBS. Leave for

about 5 min. Carefully lift the slides and place them into another grey slide holder in a white plastic

container with 1x PBS. NOTE: when lifting the slide, the coverslip should automatically come off.

Leave the slides in the prewarmed 1x PBS for another 5 min if this does not happen. Repeat with

a new batch of warm PBS if needed.

5.3. Wash three times with RT 1x PBS, ~ 3 min each.

Staining with primary antibodies

6.1. Stain with unconjugated primary antibodies o/n at +4°C diluted in Odyssey solution.

Day 3

Staining with secondary antibodies

7.1. Wash three times with 1x PBS, ~ 3 min each.

Incubate secondary antibodies 1:2000 and Hoechst 1:10000 in Odyssey buffer for 1 hr RT

7.2. Wash three times with 1x PBS x3, ~ 3 min each.

7.3. Dry the surroundings of the tissue section using soft fiber-free tissue paper. Add 100 uL of 70%

glycerol per section and carefully cover with a coverslip, avoiding air bubbles.

7.4. Scan the slides.

Note: Volume of the 70% glycerol depends on the size of the tissue, bigger tissues may need more than 100ul for example 1cm x 1cm tissue might need 120 ul of glycerol

8. Cyclic phase – repeat steps as before

8.1. Decoverslip using a prewarmed 1x PBS

8.2. Photobleach for 1.5 h, change the solution after 45 min.

8.3. Wash four times in PBS, ~ 3 min each.

8.4. Stain with pre-conjugated antibodies and Hoechst 1:10000 o/n at +4°C.

8.5. Wash three times with 1x PBS, ~ 3 min each.

8.6. Mount with 70% glycerol and scan

Repeat step 8 for the next round
